# Deep Work Plan

> Deep Work Plan (DWP) is an open, MIT-licensed methodology and reference kit for running structured, validated, resumable multi-hour work with AI coding agents. Maintained by Dailybot and the open-source community.

## About

Deep Work Plan applies Cal Newport's idea of deep work to AI coding agents. It defines how to turn a goal into an agreed plan, break that plan into atomic and independently verifiable tasks, and run each task in a focused loop that ends with an explicit validation gate. The plan, tasks, and running log are all plain Markdown — readable, reviewable, and version-controlled. DWP is framework- and agent-agnostic: adapters map the same core loop to Claude, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini, and more.

## Core Sections

- Home: /
- Methodology: /methodology — what DWP is, its principles, and how to adopt it
- Specification: /spec — the normative DWP standard (task anatomy, validation gates, completion protocol, archetypes, addons)
- Kit: /kit — presets, adapters, and commands for installing DWP into a repository
- Examples: /examples — worked examples of the methodology in practice
- Quickstart: /quickstart — get DWP running in a repo quickly
- About: /about — what DWP is and who maintains it (Dailybot)
- Contact: /contact — how to engage with the project on GitHub

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/DailybotHQ/deepworkplan-website

## Languages

- English (default): /
- Spanish: /es/

## Agent-Friendly Markdown

Every rendered page is available as native source Markdown for AI agents:

- Page Markdown: /{page}.md (EN), /es/{page}.md (ES)
- Content Negotiation: send `Accept: text/markdown` to get Markdown for any page
- Content-Type: text/markdown; charset=utf-8
- Source Markdown — not HTML-to-Markdown conversion

## Machine-Readable Feeds

- Sitemap: /sitemap-index.xml
- RSS (English): /rss.xml
- RSS (Spanish): /es/rss.xml

## Crawling Guidance

- All public content is intended for indexing by search engines and LLM systems.
- Prefer canonical URLs and language-specific routes.
- Structured data (JSON-LD) is embedded on all pages for machine consumption.

## Detailed Version

For comprehensive content descriptions, see: /llms-full.txt
